rationale: The page provides detailed information on the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), including its history, legal status, appointment process, and recent operations. It also covers the jurisdiction of the Lokpal regarding corruption allegations against various public officials and entities.
cleanedText:
Jurisdictions of Lokpal to inquire into allegations of corruption
* Prime Minister, or Minister in Union government, or Member of Parliament, and officials of Union Government under Groups A, B, C, D whether posted in India or abroad.
* Chairpersons, members, officers and directors of any board, corporation, society, trust or autonomous body either established by Act of Parliament or wholly/partly funded by Union or State government.
* Any society or trust or body that receives foreign contribution above Rs. 10 lakh.
2.2. CENTRAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION
Why in the news?
Recently, a high level committee to recommend the name of new CBI Director failed to reach consensus.
Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI)
* Genesis: Established in 1963 on the recommendation of the Santhanam Committee (1962-64).
* Administrative Control: Ministry of Personnel, Pension & Public Grievances.
* Legal Status: Non-constitutional, non-statutory body; functions under Delhi Special Police Establishment (DSPE) Act, 1946.
* Role: India's premier investigating agency; coordinates investigations on behalf of Interpol member countries.
* Motto: Industry, Impartiality and Integrity.
* Vision: Combat corruption, curb economic and violent crimes, and tackle cyber and high-technology offences.
* Organisation: Headed by a Director, assisted by Special/Additional Directors and other police ranks.
* Appointment of Director: Selected by a committee comprising the Prime Minister, Leader of Opposition, and Chief Justice of India (or a Supreme Court judge nominated by them).
* Tenure Extension: 2021 ordinances allow extension of CBI (and ED) Director's tenure up to 5 years (earlier fixed at 2 years).
* Types of Cases: Anti-corruption, economic offences, special crimes, and suo motu cases.
* Operations conducted by CBI: Operation Hawk 2025, Operation Megh Chakra 2022, and Operation Carbon 2021 related to global cybercrime network involved in online sexual exploitation of children.
